\u2014 Day 24: Multi-Signature Treasury Wallet","details":"THE \"2-OF-3 SWEET SPOT\" EXPLAINED         \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510         \u2502  2-of-2 \u2192 Maximum security. BUT: one lost key = funds locked  \u2502         \u2502           forever. Too brittle for production.                \u2502         \u2502                                                               \u2502         \u2502  2-of-3 \u2192 Industry standard (Gnosis Safe, Coinbase Vault).   \u2502         \u2502           You + Vanessa + backup key.                         \u2502         \u2502           Lose your phone? You + Vanessa sign with backup.    \u2502         \u2502           Backup compromised? You + Vanessa block attacker.   \u2502         \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518         THE WORKFLOW (3-step lifecycle of every transaction)         \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510         \u2502  1. submitTransaction  \u2192 any owner proposes a TX              \u2502         \u2502  2. confirmTransaction \u2192 each owner adds their signature      \u2502         \u2502  3. executeTransaction \u2192 any owner triggers execution once    \u2502         \u2502                          confirmationCount >= required         \u2502         \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518         MAPPING-OF-MAPPINGS (the key data structure)         isConfirmed[txId][ownerAddress] = true/false         This lets us answer \"has this specific owner confirmed this         specific TX?\" in O(1) with zero looping \u2014 gas-efficient and         secure.  Each owner can only confirm once per TX.         SAFETY: Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern throughout.         The ETH transfer in executeTransaction is the LAST operation         after all state (executed flag) is updated. This prevents         reentrancy without needing an external guard.         USE CASES IN MISTYCOIN-CORE         \u2022 Treasury: 2-of-3 with you, Vanessa, and a cold backup key.         \u2022 Protocol upgrades: require 3-of-5 from core team.         \u2022 Emergency shutdown: 4-of-5 to prevent unilateral action.","kind":"dev","methods":{"confirmTransaction(uint256)":{"details":"MAPPING-OF-MAPPINGS in action:         isConfirmed[txId][msg.sender] = true         This is O(1) \u2014 no array scanning, no gas spike as owner count grows.         After this call, transactions[txId].confirmations is incremented.         Once it hits requiredConfirmations, the TX becomes executable.","params":{"txId":"Index of the transaction to confirm."}},"constructor":{"details":"VALIDATION RULES         \u2022 No zero addresses in the owner list.         \u2022 No duplicate addresses.         \u2022 requiredConfirmations must be \u2265 1 and \u2264 owners.length.         EXAMPLE DEPLOYMENTS         \u2022 2-of-2: owners=[you, Vanessa], required=2         \u2022 2-of-3: owners=[you, Vanessa, backup], required=2 \u2190 recommended         \u2022 3-of-5: owners=[5 team members],       required=3","params":{"_owners":"Array of owner addresses.","_requiredConfirmations":"Minimum confirmations to execute a TX."}},"executeTransaction(uint256)":{"details":"SAFETY CHECK (the requirement of Day 24):         The gate is:           transactions[txId].confirmations >= requiredConfirmations         If this is false \u2192 InsufficientConfirmations() is reverted.         No amount of social pressure or manual override can bypass this \u2014         it is enforced at the EVM level.         CHECKS-EFFECTS-INTERACTIONS:         1. CHECK  \u2014 confirmations >= required, not already executed.         2. EFFECT \u2014 mark executed = true BEFORE the external call.         3. INTERACT \u2014 low-level `.call` sends ETH + data.         Marking executed BEFORE the call prevents a reentrancy attacker         from calling executeTransaction again inside the recipient's         fallback function (the TX would be marked executed \u2192 revert).","params":{"txId":"Index of the transaction to execute."}},"hasConfirmed(uint256,address)":{"details":"Direct O(1) read from the mapping-of-mappings.         isConfirmed[txId][owner] \u2014 the core data structure of a multi-sig."},"revokeConfirmation(uint256)":{"params":{"txId":"Index of the transaction to un-confirm."}},"submitTransaction(address,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"The TX starts with 0 confirmations and is NOT auto-confirmed         by the submitter \u2014 they must call confirmTransaction() too.         This is intentional: separating proposal from approval is a         security best practice (prevents social-engineering shortcuts).","params":{"data":"ABI-encoded call data. Use \"\" for plain ETH transfers.","to":"Destination address (EOA or contract).","value":"ETH to send, in wei. Can be 0 for pure data calls."},"returns":{"txId":" Index of the new transaction in the transactions array."}}},"stateVariables":{"isConfirmed":{"details":"THE MAPPING-OF-MAPPINGS:      isConfirmed[txId][ownerAddress] = true if that owner confirmed.      Tracks each owner's vote on each TX independently.      Prevents double-voting without any loop or enumeration."},"isOwner":{"details":"Quick O(1) membership check \u2014 avoids looping through `owners`."}},"title":"SimpleMultiSig \u2014 Misty Treasury \ud83d\udd10","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"confirmTransaction(uint256)":{"notice":"Add your confirmation (vote) to a pending transaction."},"confirmationsNeeded(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns how many confirmations a TX still needs before execution."},"constructor":{"notice":"Deploy the multi-sig with an initial owner set and threshold."},"executeTransaction(uint256)":{"notice":"Execute a transaction once it has enough confirmations."},"getOwners()":{"notice":"Returns the full owner list."},"getTransaction(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns all details of a specific transaction."},"getTransactionCount()":{"notice":"Returns the number of transactions ever submitted."},"hasConfirmed(uint256,address)":{"notice":"Returns whether a specific owner has confirmed a specific TX."},"owners(uint256)":{"notice":"All owners of this multi-sig wallet."},"requiredConfirmations()":{"notice":"Number of confirmations required to execute a transaction."},"revokeConfirmation(uint256)":{"notice":"Change your mind \u2014 revoke a previously given confirmation.         Useful if you spot an error in the TX details before execution."},"submitTransaction(address,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Propose a new transaction. Any owner can do this."},"transactions(uint256)":{"notice":"All proposed transactions, indexed by their txId."}},"notice":"An M-of-N multi-signature wallet. N owners hold keys; M must         confirm before any transaction can execute.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/SimpleMultiSig.sol":"SimpleMultiSig"},"evmVersion":"cancun","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"contracts/SimpleMultiSig.sol":{"keccak256":"0xcf61ad52c26af52f514aa08c30ff2f099809f8c0f05b6b1a25a2c0cb178ecace","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://ca5159bb7fb62fa4a8ed0d06a86093d8345d8e8c85d61017c63669588ced28b1","dweb:/ipfs/QmVXBtEwkKpn8r2qVGgA7y51KdSa5pxEc3SGUuErNmTRGM"]}},"version":1}

confirmTransaction(uint256).details: MAPPING-OF-MAPPINGS in action:         isConfirmed[txId][msg.sender] = true         This is O(1) — no array scanning, no gas spike as owner count grows.         After this call, transactions[txId].confirmations is incremented.         Once it hits requiredConfirmations, the TX becomes executable.

params.txId: Index of the transaction to confirm.

constructor.details: VALIDATION RULES         • No zero addresses in the owner list.         • No duplicate addresses.         • requiredConfirmations must be ≥ 1 and ≤ owners.length.         EXAMPLE DEPLOYMENTS         • 2-of-2: owners=[you, Vanessa], required=2         • 2-of-3: owners=[you, Vanessa, backup], required=2 ← recommended         • 3-of-5: owners=[5 team members],       required=3

params._requiredConfirmations: Minimum confirmations to execute a TX.

executeTransaction(uint256).details: SAFETY CHECK (the requirement of Day 24):         The gate is:           transactions[txId].confirmations >= requiredConfirmations         If this is false → InsufficientConfirmations() is reverted.         No amount of social pressure or manual override can bypass this —         it is enforced at the EVM level.         CHECKS-EFFECTS-INTERACTIONS:         1. CHECK  — confirmations >= required, not already executed.         2. EFFECT — mark executed = true BEFORE the external call.         3. INTERACT — low-level `.call` sends ETH + data.         Marking executed BEFORE the call prevents a reentrancy attacker         from calling executeTransaction again inside the recipient's         fallback function (the TX would be marked executed → revert).

params.txId: Index of the transaction to execute.

hasConfirmed(uint256,address).details: Direct O(1) read from the mapping-of-mappings.         isConfirmed[txId][owner] — the core data structure of a multi-sig.

params.txId: Index of the transaction to un-confirm.

submitTransaction(address,uint256,bytes).details: The TX starts with 0 confirmations and is NOT auto-confirmed         by the submitter — they must call confirmTransaction() too.         This is intentional: separating proposal from approval is a         security best practice (prevents social-engineering shortcuts).

params.data: ABI-encoded call data. Use "" for plain ETH transfers.
params.value: ETH to send, in wei. Can be 0 for pure data calls.

returns.txId: Index of the new transaction in the transactions array.

confirmTransaction(uint256).notice: Add your confirmation (vote) to a pending transaction.

confirmationsNeeded(uint256).notice: Returns how many confirmations a TX still needs before execution.

constructor.notice: Deploy the multi-sig with an initial owner set and threshold.

executeTransaction(uint256).notice: Execute a transaction once it has enough confirmations.

getTransaction(uint256).notice: Returns all details of a specific transaction.

getTransactionCount().notice: Returns the number of transactions ever submitted.

hasConfirmed(uint256,address).notice: Returns whether a specific owner has confirmed a specific TX.

owners(uint256).notice: All owners of this multi-sig wallet.

requiredConfirmations().notice: Number of confirmations required to execute a transaction.

revokeConfirmation(uint256).notice: Change your mind — revoke a previously given confirmation.         Useful if you spot an error in the TX details before execution.

submitTransaction(address,uint256,bytes).notice: Propose a new transaction. Any owner can do this.

transactions(uint256).notice: All proposed transactions, indexed by their txId.
